name: Easy Beef Roll-Ups
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef round steak into strips that are 1/2-inch thick.
2. Trim excess fat from the steak and remove the bone. Pound with a meat mallet or saucer to an even thickness.
3. Slice bacon into thin strips.
4. Place a slice of bacon on each steak strip, then roll up tightly.
5. Secure each roll-up with small wooden picks.
6. Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Brown the roll-ups slowly until golden brown.
7. Transfer roll-ups to a pressure cooker or heavy-duty pot.
8. Add onion soup mix and 2 cups of water to the pan, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
9. Simmer for 3 minutes, then pour liquid over the roll-ups.
10. Cook under high pressure for 10 minutes.
11. Remove the roll-ups and place them in a warm serving dish.
12. In a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch and 1/2 cup water until smooth.
13. Add this mixture to the pan with the liquid and stir constantly over low heat until thickened.
14. Pour the gravy over the roll-ups, garnish with parsley and cherry tomatoes, and serve immediately.
